sleep.name: Sleep Architecture
sleep.desc: The compounds that protect the master variable. Circadian support, GABA modulation, and core temperature regulation.

items.name: Magnesium L-Threonate
items.why: Sleep stack anchor. Combined with Apigenin and Glycine, this is the exact recommended pre-sleep protocol. At your training volume, magnesium depletion through sweat is real.
science: Only magnesium form shown to cross the blood-brain barrier effectively
Supports GABA signaling, reduces sleep onset latency
80% of Americans are magnesium deficient

sources.title: Magnesium L-threonate enhances learning and memory (original study)
Magnesium and sleep quality (systematic review)
L-threonate form premium pricing vs standard magnesium forms (cost-effectiveness analysis)
Examine.com analysis: most magnesium sleep benefits apply to deficient populations

items.rationale: Heavy training + sweat + deficit increases depletion risk. L-threonate specifically targets the CNS.
items.impact: Sleep onset latency, deep sleep %, cognitive clarity
items.watching: Eight Sleep score on adherent vs non-adherent nights

items.name: Glycine
items.why: Beautiful synergy with Eight Sleep — both work by lowering core temperature. Collagen synthesis bonus for joint health during heavy training.
science: 2-3g before bed lowers core body temperature
Primary physiological trigger for sleep onset
Synergistic with Eight Sleep cooling

sources.title: Glycine ingestion improves subjective sleep quality (original RCT)
Glycine-induced hypothermia and sleep regulation
Effect size on sleep is modest in non-deficient populations

items.rationale: Synergistic with Eight Sleep cooling — both lower core temp via different mechanisms.
items.impact: Core temp drop, sleep onset latency, deep sleep %

items.name: Apigenin
items.why: Gentlest member of the sleep stack — best risk/reward ratio. If you take one sleep supplement, this is it.
science: Flavonoid that modulates GABA-A receptors
Reduces sleep onset latency and anxiety without grogginess
No tolerance, no dependence

sources.title: Apigenin as a GABA-A receptor modulator (preclinical)
Chamomile extract (apigenin) for generalized anxiety disorder
Limited human clinical trials — most evidence is preclinical

items.rationale: Best risk/reward in the sleep stack — extremely safe with plausible mechanism.
items.impact: Sleep onset latency, subjective sleep quality

items.name: L-Theanine
items.why: Monitor your response — if Eight Sleep shows no difference or worse sleep on Theanine nights, candidate for removal.
science: Promotes alpha brain waves, reduces anxiety without sedation
Synergistic with caffeine during the day
Some people get disruptive vivid dreams — individual response varies

sources.title: L-theanine promotes relaxation without drowsiness
Effects of L-theanine on stress and sleep quality
Vivid dreams may disrupt sleep architecture

items.rationale: Monitoring via Eight Sleep — data will determine whether this stays.
items.impact: Subjective anxiety, sleep onset
items.questionReason: Individual response varies — vivid dreams may disrupt sleep for some

muscle.name: Muscle & Performance
muscle.desc: Protecting lean mass during a 1500 kcal deficit. Every gram of muscle preserved is metabolic insurance.

items.name: Creatine Monohydrate
items.why: During deficit, creatine is protective — helps preserve the muscle you're working to keep. 5g/day, no loading, no cycling.
science: Most studied sports supplement in history
Increases phosphocreatine stores for high-intensity performance
Emerging cognitive benefits — neuroprotective properties
Supports lean mass retention during caloric deficit

sources.title: ISSN Position Stand: Creatine Supplementation
Creatine and creatinine metabolism (physiological review)
Long-term safety concerns and kidney function (review)

items.rationale: Most extensively studied supplement in history with consistent positive results across hundreds of RCTs.
items.impact: Strength performance, lean mass on DEXA, cognitive clarity

items.name: Protein Supplement
items.why: At 1800 cal with 190g protein target, you need ~42% calories from protein. A shake is often the only way to close the gap.
science: Whey/casein supports 190g daily target
Most calorie-efficient protein source (120-150 cal for 30g)
Critical for lean mass preservation during deficit

sources.title: Protein supplementation and lean mass (meta-analysis)
Dietary protein and muscle mass in older adults
Whole food protein sources superior to supplements for muscle protein synthesis

items.rationale: At 190g target in 1800 cal, hitting protein without a shake requires extreme food selection.
items.impact: MacroFactor protein hit rate, lean mass retention

items.name: Electrolytes
items.why: Deficit + hard training + 3L water + sweating = quadruple depletion vector. Fatigue and headaches may be electrolyte-driven, not calorie-driven.
science: Sodium, potassium, magnesium depleted through sweat and restriction
Most people training in deficit are electrolyte depleted
Symptoms: fatigue, cramps, headaches, reduced performance

sources.title: Sodium and fluid replacement during prolonged exercise
ACSM position stand: exercise and fluid replacement
Electrolyte supplementation unnecessary for most recreational exercisers

items.rationale: Deficit + training + hydration = quadruple depletion vector. Symptoms resolve immediately on supplementation.
items.impact: Training performance, energy, cramp frequency

items.name: Collagen Peptides
items.why: Losing 117 lbs means significant skin adaptation. Joint protection during high training volume is secondary benefit.
science: 15g + vitamin C 30-60 min before exercise increases collagen synthesis
Supports joint cartilage, tendon health, skin elasticity
Baar research on tendon/ligament collagen synthesis

sources.title: Vitamin C-enriched gelatin supplementation increases collagen synthesis (Baar lab)
Collagen peptides improve skin elasticity (RCT)
Systematic review found inconsistent evidence for joint health claims

items.rationale: Losing 117 lbs creates unique skin/joint demands — Baar research on pre-exercise collagen + vitamin C is specific.
items.impact: Joint comfort, skin elasticity during weight loss

items.name: L-Glutamine
items.why: At 190g protein/day, supplemental benefit is likely marginal. Keep if GI comfort is noticeably better.
science: Supports gut barrier integrity and immune function
Most abundant amino acid
At high protein intake, likely getting adequate from food

sources.title: Glutamine supplementation and gut barrier function
Glutamine and immune function during exercise (review)
At adequate protein intake (>1.6g/kg), supplemental glutamine provides no measurable benefit (meta-analysis)

items.rationale: Only remains for GI comfort — monitoring for removal.
items.impact: GI comfort, immune function
items.questionReason: At 190g protein/day, likely redundant — candidate for 'do I need this?'

cognitive.name: Cognitive Support
cognitive.desc: Neuroplasticity, focus, and the cognitive infrastructure.

items.name: Lion's Mane
items.pausedReason: Paused — zero tracking entries after 31 days
items.why: Cognitive performance matters. Emerging evidence — don't stress if missed.
science: Stimulates nerve growth factor (NGF) synthesis
Supports neuroplasticity and cognitive function
Not yet confirmed in large RCTs

sources.title: Lion's mane stimulates nerve growth factor synthesis (preclinical)
Hericium erinaceus improves mild cognitive impairment (small RCT)
No large-scale human RCTs — most evidence is preclinical or small samples

items.rationale: Paused due to zero adherence tracking. Good N=1 candidate if resumed with measurement.
items.impact: Subjective cognitive clarity, focus during deep work
items.questionReason: Emerging evidence only — good N=1 candidate

items.name: Cordyceps
items.pausedReason: Paused — zero tracking entries after 31 days
items.why: Theoretically supports the aerobic engine during Zone 2. Evidence is thin. Good N=1 candidate.
science: May improve oxygen utilization and aerobic performance
Supports ATP production
Adaptogenic properties — evidence promising but limited

sources.title: Cordyceps supplementation improves exercise performance (systematic review)
Cordyceps militaris improves tolerance to high-intensity exercise
Human trials show inconsistent results for aerobic performance

items.rationale: Paused. If resumed, HR recovery data would be the validation metric.
items.impact: Aerobic performance, HR recovery, Zone 2 efficiency
items.watching: HR recovery trend on vs off weeks
items.questionReason: Evidence is thin — track HR recovery as validation metric

items.name: Reishi
items.pausedReason: Paused — zero tracking entries after 31 days
items.why: Lowest-evidence mushroom supplement. Consider whether cost and pill burden is justified.
science: Adaptogenic mushroom with immunomodulatory properties
Traditional use for sleep and stress
Limited clinical evidence

sources.title: Ganoderma lucidum (reishi) immunomodulatory properties
Reishi for fatigue and quality of life (small trial)
Clinical evidence is extremely limited — mostly traditional medicine claims

items.rationale: Lowest evidence in entire stack. Paused indefinitely.
items.impact: Subjective calm, sleep quality
items.questionReason: Lowest evidence in the stack — candidate for pausing

longevity.name: Longevity Foundation
longevity.desc: The long-game supplements. Cardiovascular protection, immune resilience, and micronutrient insurance.

items.name: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
items.why: Genome shows cardiovascular risk markers. Omega-3 is most likely to move your TG/HDL ratio over time.
science: Reduces systemic inflammation, improves TG/HDL ratio
Supports cell membrane fluidity, cardioprotective
EPA specifically has antidepressant effects at 1g+ doses

sources.title: Omega-3 fatty acids and cardiovascular disease (Cochrane Review)
VITAL trial: n-3 fatty acids and cardiovascular events

items.rationale: Genome-confirmed FADS2 variant makes pre-formed EPA/DHA essential — plant ALA conversion is impaired ~30%.
items.impact: Triglycerides, TG/HDL ratio, hs-CRP, mood stability
items.watching: Lipid panel trends across lab draws

items.name: Vitamin D3 + K2
items.why: Living in Seattle makes this non-optional. Lab trends track 25-OH Vitamin D to confirm range.
science: Essential for calcium metabolism, immune function, mood
Deficiency 40-50% at northern latitudes
Seattle (47°N) produces zero cutaneous vitamin D Oct–Mar
Target: 40-60 ng/mL

sources.title: Vitamin D supplementation and musculoskeletal health (meta-analysis)
VITAL trial: Vitamin D and cancer/CVD prevention

items.rationale: Living at 47°N latitude with zero cutaneous synthesis Oct–Mar makes this non-negotiable — lab-confirmed.
items.impact: 25-OH Vitamin D lab levels, immune function, mood

items.name: Zinc Picolinate
items.why: During deficit + heavy training, zinc depletion through sweat is real. Supports immune resilience.
science: Supports immune function, testosterone production
Athletes lose zinc through sweat
Picolinate has superior bioavailability
Excess (>40mg) can deplete copper

sources.title: Zinc supplementation and immune function (meta-analysis)
Zinc status and exercise performance
Excess zinc depletes copper — balance is critical

items.rationale: Genome-confirmed SLC39A4 variant reduces absorption. Serum zinc validates the need.
items.impact: Immune resilience, testosterone, zinc lab levels

items.name: NAC (N-Acetyl Cysteine)
items.why: Glutathione support during deficit and high training load. Behavioral benefit (reducing rumination) may support discipline stack.
science: Glutathione precursor — body's master antioxidant
Supports liver detoxification, reduces oxidative stress
Evidence for reducing rumination/compulsive behavior

sources.title: NAC as a glutathione precursor and antioxidant (review)
NAC reduces oxidative stress and supports liver function
Some evidence NAC may blunt exercise adaptations (antioxidant interference)

items.rationale: Glutathione support during deficit outweighs theoretical adaptation blunting — liver panels will validate.
items.impact: Liver function markers (AST, ALT), oxidative stress

items.name: Multivitamin
items.why: At 1800 cal/day, eating significantly less food than your body is sized for. Insurance policy.
science: During restriction, micronutrient gaps more likely
Fills selenium, manganese, trace mineral gaps
Evidence in well-nourished populations is weak
During 1500 kcal deficit the insurance argument is stronger

sources.title: Multivitamin supplementation and micronutrient adequacy during caloric restriction
COSMOS trial: multivitamins and cognitive function in older adults
USPSTF finds insufficient evidence for multivitamins in well-nourished adults

items.rationale: At 1800 cal deficit, 'well-nourished' doesn't apply — micronutrient insurance argument is stronger.
items.impact: Micronutrient sufficiency

items.name: B Complex
items.why: Genome includes MTHFR variants — check whether you need methylated forms. Homocysteine is the validation metric.
science: Cofactors in energy metabolism, methylation, neurotransmitter synthesis
Depletion risk increases during caloric restriction
B12 and folate important for homocysteine management

sources.title: B vitamins and homocysteine metabolism (review)
B vitamin supplementation and cognitive function
Excess B6 can cause peripheral neuropathy

items.rationale: MTHFR variants in genome suggest methylation support needed — homocysteine is the validation metric.
items.impact: Homocysteine levels, energy, cognitive clarity

metabolic.name: Metabolic & Body Comp
metabolic.desc: Supporting the deficit engine. Glucose management, fat oxidation, and gut health.

items.name: Myo-Inositol
items.why: During metabolic transformation, insulin sensitivity is a key lever. CGM data should validate.
science: Supports insulin signaling and serotonin receptor sensitivity
Anxiolytic properties
Evidence for metabolic benefit in insulin-resistant populations

sources.title: Myo-inositol improves insulin sensitivity (meta-analysis)
Inositol and glucose metabolism in metabolic syndrome
Most positive evidence is in PCOS populations — limited data in metabolically healthy males

items.rationale: CGM data will validate whether glucose disposal improves — clear measurable outcome.
items.impact: Glucose variability, insulin sensitivity via CGM

items.name: Green Tea Phytosome (EGCG)
items.why: Marginal fat oxidation support. Insurance, not primary lever. Weight trend won't show it.
science: EGCG supports fat oxidation and thermogenesis
Antioxidant properties
Modest effect on fat loss (1-2 lbs over 12 weeks)

sources.title: Green tea catechins and fat oxidation (meta-analysis)
EGCG and thermogenesis in humans
Effect size on fat loss is 1-2 lbs over 12 weeks — clinically meaningless

items.rationale: Insurance play only — wouldn't supplement for fat loss alone.
items.impact: Marginal fat oxidation, antioxidant status
items.questionReason: Effect size too small to measure — pure insurance play

items.name: Probiotics
items.why: During high-protein deficit, GI comfort matters for adherence. Consider fermented foods instead.
science: Gut microbiome correlates with metabolic health and mood
Evidence is strain-specific and mixed
Fermented foods may be superior for diversity

sources.title: Probiotics and gut microbiome modulation (systematic review)
Probiotics for GI health during dietary restriction
Probiotic supplementation may reduce gut microbiome diversity vs fermented foods

items.rationale: Candidate for replacement with fermented food strategy.
items.impact: GI comfort, digestion quality
items.watching: GI comfort changes on vs off
items.questionReason: Candidate for reassessment — fermented foods may be food-first approach

genome_snps.note: Reduces Vitamin D receptor binding efficiency — requires 5,000 IU/day. Standard 600–2,000 IU is insufficient for this genotype.
Impairs ALA→EPA/DHA conversion (delta-6 desaturase reduced ~30%). Pre-formed fish oil is essential — plant ALA sources are ineffective.
ZIP4 transporter variant reduces intestinal zinc absorption. Serum zinc runs low without supplementation despite adequate dietary intake.
